comment_text.sn15.20:2.3: For Vepulla, see SN 15.10:2.2. | A pācīnavaṁsa was a hall or shed built for the ritual, with bamboo (vaṁsa) beams running easterly (pācīna; Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 3.1.1.6 and Eggeling’s note). Perhaps the mountain was noted as the site for the introduction of this ritual institution. In the Buddha’s day, there was a “Park of the East-Raftered Hall” in Cetī (MN 128:8.1, AN 8.30:1.2).
comment_text.sn15.20:2.4: Tivara may be connected to Vedic trivarūtha, the “triple defence”. This may be connected with the fortifications for which Rājagaha is famous. But by Vedic times it acquired a religious sense, here suggesting the people who have the protection of the gods due to their correct performance of the ritual in their hall. The “triple defence” can be seen as a precursor of the Buddhist triple refuge.
comment_text.sn15.20:2.5: For the long lifespans of humans in the past, see AN 7.74:9.1. Declining lifespans are charted at DN 26:14.2.
comment_text.sn15.20:2.7: Kakusandha and the other Buddhas are mentioned at SN 12.7–10. His lifespan of 40,000 years is confirmed at DN 14:1.7.4.
comment_text.sn15.20:2.8: The pairs of disciples are listed at DN 14:1.9.1. A story of Sañjīva is given at MN 50:9.2.
comment_text.sn15.20:3.2: These two terms are found in Rig Veda 4.6.9, “red horses going straight, going well” (rohitāsa ṛjvañcaḥ svañcaḥ), where rohitassa is the “red horse” of the sunbeams (SN 2.26), and Sanskrit vañca is evidently found in Pali vaṅka in the sense “going” (unless it is from vañcati, “fire”). Perhaps the mountain was named for the flashing beams of sunlight that broke over it at dawn.
comment_text.sn15.20:4.1: Supassa is “Fairslope”, a name used for several mountains, including one on the eastern side of mount Meru.
comment_text.sn15.20:4.5: Stories of Kassapa, the Buddha immediately preceding Gotama, are found at MN 81 and AN 5.180.His lifespan is given at DN 14:1.7.6,
comment_text.sn15.20:5.5: Kauṭilya says there are fifteen muhuttas (Sanskrit muhūrta) per day, and fifteen per night, making 48 minutes (Arthaśāstra 2.20.37).

root_text.sn15.20:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.sn15.20:2.2: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.20:2.3: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, imassa vepullassa pabbatassa ‘pācīnavaṁso’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:2.4: Tena kho pana, bhikkhave, samayena manussānaṁ ‘tivarā’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:2.5: Tivarānaṁ, bhikkhave, manussānaṁ cattārīsa vassasahassāni āyuppamāṇaṁ ahosi.
root_text.sn15.20:2.6: Tivarā, bhikkhave, manussā pācīnavaṁsaṁ pabbataṁ catūhena ārohanti, catūhena orohanti.
root_text.sn15.20:2.7: Tena kho pana, bhikkhave, samayena kakusandho bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho loke uppanno hoti.
root_text.sn15.20:2.8: Kakusandhassa, bhikkhave, bhagavato arahato sammāsambuddhassa vidhurasañjīvaṁ nāma sāvakayugaṁ ahosi aggaṁ bhaddayugaṁ.
root_text.sn15.20:2.9: Passatha, bhikkhave, sā cevimassa pabbatassa samaññā antarahitā, te ca manussā kālaṅkatā, so ca bhagavā parinibbuto.
root_text.sn15.20:2.13: Yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhave, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccituṁ.
root_text.sn15.20:3.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, imassa vepullassa pabbatassa ‘vaṅkako’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:3.2: Tena kho pana, bhikkhave, samayena manussānaṁ ‘rohitassā’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:3.3: Rohitassānaṁ, bhikkhave, manussānaṁ tiṁsavassasahassāni āyuppamāṇaṁ ahosi.
root_text.sn15.20:3.4: Rohitassā, bhikkhave, manussā vaṅkakaṁ pabbataṁ tīhena ārohanti, tīhena orohanti.
root_text.sn15.20:3.5: Tena kho pana, bhikkhave, samayena koṇāgamano bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho loke uppanno hoti.
root_text.sn15.20:3.6: Koṇāgamanassa, bhikkhave, bhagavato arahato sammāsambuddhassa bhiyyosuttaraṁ nāma sāvakayugaṁ ahosi aggaṁ bhaddayugaṁ.
root_text.sn15.20:3.7: Passatha, bhikkhave, sā cevimassa pabbatassa samaññā antarahitā, te ca manussā kālaṅkatā, so ca bhagavā parinibbuto.
root_text.sn15.20:4.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, imassa vepullassa pabbatassa ‘supasso’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:4.2: Tena kho pana, bhikkhave, samayena manussānaṁ ‘suppiyā’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:4.3: Suppiyānaṁ, bhikkhave, manussānaṁ vīsativassasahassāni āyuppamāṇaṁ ahosi.
root_text.sn15.20:4.4: Suppiyā, bhikkhave, manussā supassaṁ pabbataṁ dvīhena ārohanti, dvīhena orohanti.
root_text.sn15.20:4.5: Tena kho pana, bhikkhave, samayena kassapo bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho loke uppanno hoti.
root_text.sn15.20:4.6: Kassapassa, bhikkhave, bhagavato arahato sammāsambuddhassa tissabhāradvājaṁ nāma sāvakayugaṁ ahosi aggaṁ bhaddayugaṁ.
root_text.sn15.20:4.7: Passatha, bhikkhave, sā cevimassa pabbatassa samaññā antarahitā, te ca manussā kālaṅkatā, so ca bhagavā parinibbuto.
root_text.sn15.20:5.1: Etarahi kho pana, bhikkhave, imassa vepullassa pabbatassa ‘vepullo’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:5.2: Etarahi kho pana, bhikkhave, imesaṁ manussānaṁ ‘māgadhakā’tveva samaññā udapādi.
root_text.sn15.20:5.3: Māgadhakānaṁ, bhikkhave, manussānaṁ appakaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ parittaṁ lahukaṁ;
root_text.sn15.20:5.4: yo ciraṁ jīvati so vassasataṁ appaṁ vā bhiyyo.
root_text.sn15.20:5.5: Māgadhakā, bhikkhave, manussā vepullaṁ pabbataṁ muhuttena ārohanti muhuttena orohanti.
root_text.sn15.20:5.6: Etarahi kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho loke uppanno.
root_text.sn15.20:5.7: Mayhaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, sāriputtamoggallānaṁ nāma sāvakayugaṁ aggaṁ bhaddayugaṁ.
root_text.sn15.20:5.8: Bhavissati, bhikkhave, so samayo yā ayañcevimassa pabbatassa samaññā antaradhāyissati, ime ca manussā kālaṁ karissanti, ahañca parinibbāyissāmi.
root_text.sn15.20:5.12: Yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhave, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.
root_text.sn15.20:6.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn15.20:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.sn15.20:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn15.20:2.1: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.2: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.3: Once upon a time, mendicants, this Mount Vepulla was known as Pācīnavaṁsa.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.4: And at that time people were known as Tivaras.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.5: The lifespan of the Tivaras was 40,000 years.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.6: It took them four days to climb Mount Vepulla, and four days to descend.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.7: At that time Kakusandha, the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha arose in the world.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.8: Kakusandha had a fine pair of chief disciples named Vidhura and Sañjīva.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.9: See, mendicants! This mountain’s name has vanished, those people have passed away, and that Buddha has become fully quenched.
translation_text.sn15.20:2.13: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.1: Once upon a time this Mount Vepulla was known as Vaṅkaka.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.2: And at that time people were known as Rohitassas.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.3: The lifespan of the Rohitassas was 30,000 years.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.4: It took them three days to climb Mount Vepulla, and three days to descend.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.5: At that time Koṇāgamana, the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha arose in the world.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.6: Koṇāgamana had a fine pair of chief disciples named Bhiyyosa and Uttara.
translation_text.sn15.20:3.7: See, mendicants! This mountain’s name has vanished, those people have passed away, and that Buddha has become fully quenched.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.1: Once upon a time this Mount Vepulla was known as Supassa.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.2: And at that time people were known as Suppiyas.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.3: The lifespan of the Suppiyas was 20,000 years.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.4: It took them two days to climb Mount Vepulla, and two days to descend.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.5: At that time Kassapa, the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha arose in the world.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.6: Kassapa had a fine pair of chief disciples named Tissa and Bhāradvāja.
translation_text.sn15.20:4.7: See, mendicants! This mountain’s name has vanished, those people have passed away, and that Buddha has become fully quenched.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.1: These days this Mount Vepulla is known as Vepulla.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.2: And these people are known as Magadhans.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.3: The lifespan of the Magadhans is short, brief, and fleeting.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.4: A long life is a hundred years or a little more.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.5: It takes the Magadhans an hour to climb Mount Vepulla, and an hour to descend.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.6: And now I am the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha who has arisen in the world.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.7: I have a fine pair of chief disciples named Sāriputta and Moggallāna.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.8: There will come a time when this mountain’s name will vanish, those people will die, and I will be fully extinguished.
translation_text.sn15.20:5.12: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
translation_text.sn15.20:6.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn15.20:6.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn15.20:8.2: their nature is to rise and fall;
translation_text.sn15.20:9.5: The Linked Discourses on the unknown beginning are complete.
